question: Considering Ch.01 plant overview, Ch.05 Table 5.1-2 flow data, Figure 5.1-2 flow path, and Ch.05 text on natural circulation, does the overall NuScale thermal-hydraulic design provide adequate safety margins?

nuscale_ch01_0058.title: Steam Generator Thermal-Hydraulic Performance Testing - Electrically Heated Facility
nuscale_ch01_0058.summary: This section describes the critical heat flux (CHF) testing conducted for the NuScale NuFuel HTP2™ fuel design using an electrically heated 5x5 simulated fuel bundle with AREVA HTP™/HMP™ grid technology. The tests, performed at the AREVA Karlstein Thermal Hydraulics facility, aimed to gather CHF data, validate CHF correlations, determine mixing coefficients, and measure pressure drop characteristics under various hydraulic conditions to support the NuScale reactor safety analysis.

nuscale_ch01_0044.title: Chemical and Volume Control System
nuscale_ch01_0044.summary: The Chemical and Volume Control System section describes the NuScale reactor’s closed-loop, two-phase natural circulation cooling system, which uses two independent trains of decay heat removal equipment, each capable of fully cooling the reactor. In case of a steam generator tube failure, the system isolates the affected generator and continues cooling through the intact one, transferring heat to a large, stainless steel-lined ultimate heat sink pool that ensures passive heat removal even during a total loss of AC power.

nuscale_ch01_0738.title: SRP 15.4.3, Rev 3: Control Rod Misoperation (System Malfunction or Operator Error)II.1 Thermal Margin Limits
nuscale_ch01_0738.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for control rod misoperation events, focusing on thermal margin limits. NuScale applies appropriate heat generation rate limits and uses the 95/95 MCHFR approach to ensure fuel and cladding integrity, noting that certain criteria are not applicable due to differences in reactor design. The terminology and analysis methods are tailored to NuScale’s technology, and guidance for some events is addressed in other sections due to design differences from traditional PWRs and BWRs.

nuscale_ch01_0732.title: SRP 15.4.1, Rev 3: Uncontrolled Control Rod Assembly Withdrawal From a Subcritical or Low Power Startup ConditionII.1.A Thermal Margin Limits
nuscale_ch01_0732.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for various reactor accident scenarios, highlighting that its passive natural circulation design eliminates the need for reactor coolant pumps, making certain SRP sections not applicable. For uncontrolled control rod withdrawal events, NuScale aligns with SRP 15.4.1 by using critical heat flux (CHF) and heat generation rate limits to ensure thermal margin and fuel integrity.

nuscale_ch01_0059.title: Steam Generator Thermal-Hydraulic Performance Testing - Fluid-Heated Facility
nuscale_ch01_0059.summary: The section describes thermal-hydraulic performance testing of the NuScale steam generator using an electrically-heated facility at SIET in Italy. The tests, which included various heating zones and types (adiabatic, diabatic, transient, and density wave oscillation), allowed precise control and measurement of heat flux and pressure dynamics to support design validation, code benchmarking, and regulatory inspection. The results informed steam generator design, flow restrictor sizing, and assessment of flow-induced vibration risks.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain fuel integrity, minimize solid deposits, and ensure uniform boron concentration under all operating conditions.

nuscale_ch01_0069.title: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
nuscale_ch01_0069.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation and control (I&C), and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) to visually clarify design information. It lists the relevant diagrams and legends used for both I&C and piping and instrumentation diagrams, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be provided by COL applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: This section describes the evaluation of the NuScale Power Plant’s system performance, focusing on optimizing steady-state parameters such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures to maximize electrical generation and maintain safety margins. Calculations using thermal hydraulic software and hand verification establish best-estimate, maximum, and minimum flow conditions, accounting for uncertainties and ensuring the natural circulation design is supported by low pressure losses in the reactor coolant system.

nuscale_ch01_0057.title: Critical Heat Flux Testing - NuFuel HTP2™ Fuel Design
nuscale_ch01_0057.summary: This section describes the critical heat flux (CHF) testing conducted for the NuFuel HTP2™ fuel design, which uses AREVA HMP™/HTP™ spacer grid technology. The testing aimed to gather CHF data to develop and validate NuScale's CHF correlations, as well as to measure subchannel temperatures, mixing coefficients, and pressure drop characteristics under various hydraulic conditions using a 5x5 simulated fuel bundle at the AREVA Karlstein facility.

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes special plant features of the NuScale Power Plant, highlighting structures such as the Central Utility Building and Diesel Generator Buildings, which house essential but nonsafety-related systems and equipment. It also emphasizes human factors engineering, noting that the plant design minimizes human error through automation, digital controls, and optimized workload management, in compliance with regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0066.title: Material Referenced
nuscale_ch01_0066.summary: This section lists the topical and technical reports incorporated by reference in the NuScale Power Plant Design Certification Application, as detailed in Tables 1.6-1 and 1.6-2. These reports cover various aspects of the plant’s safety analysis, including quality assurance, risk significance, safety classification, accident analysis methodologies, and nuclear analysis codes.

nuscale_ch01_0815.title: DSRS 15.9.A, Rev 0: Thermal Hydraulic Stability Review ResponsibilitiesII.9 Stability-related instrumentation functionality should be demonstrated by analysis.
nuscale_ch01_0815.summary: The NuScale design does not use the exclusion zone or detect-and-suppress (D&S) methods for thermal hydraulic stability, as existing reactor trip signals already prevent conditions that could lead to flow instabilities. The system partially conforms to some regulatory criteria by ensuring reactor trips occur before safety limits are violated, but does not employ D&S systems or backup options, as these are deemed unnecessary due to the design’s inherent protections.

nuscale_ch01_0812.title: DSRS 15.9.A, Rev 0: Thermal Hydraulic Stability Review ResponsibilitiesII.6 Backup options if licensing solutions declared inoperable
nuscale_ch01_0812.summary: The NuScale design does not use exclusion zone or detect-and-suppress (D&S) methods for thermal hydraulic stability, as reactor trip signals act before unstable flow oscillations can develop. Backup options for licensing solutions are not required because existing technical specifications for the Reactor Trip System (RTS) already control allowable unavailabilities of protective trips, ensuring safety limits are not violated.

nuscale_ch01_0065.title: References
nuscale_ch01_0065.summary: This section lists references related to the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, specifically requirements for additional technical information. It cites a NuScale Power document on critical heat flux correlations as a key reference.

edges.description: Section A provides critical heat flux (CHF) test data and correlations for the NuScale fuel design, which supports the safety margin assessment of the thermal-hydraulic design described in Section B's overview of the plant's natural circulation and decay heat removal systems.

Section A provides critical heat flux (CHF) test data and correlations specific to the NuScale fuel design, which are used to demonstrate that the design maintains adequate thermal margins as required by the regulatory thermal margin limits specified in Section B.

Section A provides experimental CHF data and correlations specific to the NuScale fuel design, which are used to demonstrate that the design meets the thermal margin limits and regulatory requirements specified in Section B.

Section A describes the objective and results of critical heat flux (CHF) testing for the NuScale fuel design, while Section B details the methodology of the electrically-heated steam generator performance tests that provided the controlled heat flux boundary conditions necessary for obtaining the CHF data referenced in Section A.

Section A provides critical heat flux (CHF) test data and correlations specific to the NuScale fuel design, which are necessary to validate and support the natural circulation-based reactor coolant system described in Section B as part of the overall thermal-hydraulic safety margin analysis.

Section A describes the design features of the NuScale Chemical and Volume Control System that enable passive decay heat removal, which supports the safety margins required by the regulatory thermal margin limits specified in Section B.

Section A describes the design features and operation of the NuScale Chemical and Volume Control System, specifically its passive decay heat removal capability, while Section B outlines the regulatory requirements that the DHRS must be safety grade and automatically initiated, thus Section A's design features are intended to satisfy the regulatory requirements specified in Section B.

Section B describes thermal-hydraulic performance testing of steam generator tubes under controlled conditions, which provides empirical evidence supporting the operational claims about decay heat removal and natural circulation described in Section A.

Section A describes specific safety features and operational responses of the Chemical and Volume Control System that implement the general natural circulation-based design basis outlined in Section B, connecting the detailed system operation to the overall thermal-hydraulic safety strategy.

Section A describes NuScale's conformance with SRP thermal margin limits for control rod misoperation scenarios, while Section B outlines similar conformance for uncontrolled control rod withdrawal and related safety system requirements, both referencing the adequacy of thermal margin limits as a key safety criterion.

Section B provides experimental evidence of steam generator thermal-hydraulic performance under controlled conditions, which supports the regulatory compliance claims regarding thermal margin limits described in Section A.

Section A outlines the regulatory thermal margin limits and criteria that the NuScale design must satisfy, while Section B describes the design basis of the reactor coolant system, including its reliance on natural circulation, which is the operational feature evaluated against those regulatory limits.
Section B provides experimental evidence on steam generator thermal-hydraulic performance, which supports the regulatory thermal margin limits and safety requirements outlined in Section A.

Section A specifies regulatory thermal margin limits and safety requirements that the NuScale design must meet, while Section B describes the design basis of the reactor coolant system, including its reliance on natural circulation, which is directly relevant to demonstrating compliance with those regulatory limits.

Section A provides detailed test data on steam generator thermal-hydraulic performance under controlled conditions, which supports the general design basis in Section B that relies on natural circulation for reactor coolant flow.

Section A provides visual and schematic details of the plant's instrumentation and control systems, which are referenced in Section B's thermal-hydraulic performance testing to contextualize and validate the test setup and results.

Section A provides detailed drawings and diagrams that visually clarify the design and operation of systems described in Section B, such as the decay heat removal and natural circulation features of the Chemical and Volume Control System.

Section A provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify the NuScale plant's design, which support the evaluation of whether the thermal-hydraulic design (including safety margins) conforms to the regulatory thermal margin limits described in Section B.

Section A discusses the establishment of steady state thermal-hydraulic parameters for optimal plant performance, while Section B provides critical heat flux (CHF) testing data used to validate the safety margins of those parameters, thus Section B supports the adequacy claims made in Section A.

Section A describes the system evaluation criteria for optimizing NuScale plant performance, while Section B details the configuration and redundancy of the decay heat removal system, with both sections connected by their focus on ensuring adequate heat removal and safety margins through system design and operational criteria.

Section A describes how the NuScale system establishes steady-state thermal-hydraulic parameters to optimize performance and safety, while Section B outlines the regulatory thermal margin limits (such as fuel centerline temperature and cladding strain) that the design must satisfy, connecting them through the requirement that the system's design results must meet these regulatory safety criteria.

Section A provides detailed drawings and diagrams that visually clarify the system components and flow paths, which support the system evaluation and performance criteria discussed in Section B by illustrating how the design achieves the described operational capabilities.

Section A's critical heat flux testing data and correlations for the NuFuel HTP2™ fuel design are prerequisites for Section B's steam generator thermal-hydraulic performance testing, as accurate CHF correlations are necessary to evaluate and validate the safety margins in the overall thermal-hydraulic design.

Section A provides critical heat flux test data and correlations for the NuScale fuel design, which are necessary to validate the thermal-hydraulic performance and safety margins described in Section B's overview of the plant's cooling systems and their ability to remove decay heat under natural circulation.

Section A provides critical heat flux test data and correlations specific to the NuScale fuel design, which are used to demonstrate that the design meets the thermal margin limits and safety criteria specified in Section B's regulatory requirements.

Section B provides critical heat flux testing data and correlations that support the validation of the NuScale fuel design's thermal-hydraulic performance, which is a technical underpinning for the plant features and systems described in Section A.

Section A describes the Central Utility Building's support systems, some of which (like chemical treatment equipment) are directly related to the Chemical and Volume Control System detailed in Section B, which manages reactor coolant chemistry and supports safe thermal-hydraulic operation.

Section A describes general plant features and utility systems, while Section B outlines specific regulatory thermal margin limits that the plant's thermal-hydraulic design must satisfy, connecting them through the requirement that plant features support compliance with these safety margins.

Section A lists topical reports, including those on quality assurance, that are referenced in the FSAR, while Section B describes a specific test program (CHF testing) whose results and correlations may be among the technical information referenced in Section A.

Section A lists referenced topical reports, including those that describe quality assurance and technical bases, which provide supporting documentation for the general plant description and safety system functions detailed in Section B.

Section A lists topical reports, including those detailing quality assurance, that are referenced to support the NuScale FSAR's conformance claims in Section B regarding thermal margin limits and regulatory criteria for safety analysis.

Section B provides experimental CHF data and correlation validation that supports Section A's requirement to demonstrate, by analysis, that the NuScale design's instrumentation and protective actions prevent violation of CHF limits, thus ensuring thermal hydraulic stability.

Section B describes the NuScale plant's closed-loop, two-phase natural circulation cooling system and its passive safety features, which Section A references as part of the design's approach to thermal hydraulic stability and the avoidance of unstable flow oscillations, thereby supporting the regulatory requirement for stability-related instrumentation functionality.

Section A describes the NuScale design's approach to thermal hydraulic stability and instrumentation, while Section B outlines the thermal margin limits and fuel safety criteria that the design must meet, with both sections connected by their focus on ensuring that the plant's thermal-hydraulic performance maintains adequate safety margins under various operational and malfunction scenarios.

Section B provides critical heat flux (CHF) test data and correlation validation that support Section A's claim that reactor trip signals prevent violation of CHF limits, thereby ensuring thermal-hydraulic stability and safety margins.

Section A discusses the regulatory requirements and protective actions for thermal hydraulic stability, specifically noting that NuScale's design avoids unstable flow oscillations by reactor trip signals, while Section B describes the plant's closed-loop, two-phase natural circulation cooling system and its passive safety features, together showing that the design's operational features (B) are intended to satisfy the regulatory safety requirements (A).

Section A describes the NuScale design's approach to thermal hydraulic stability and how reactor trip signals prevent exceeding critical heat flux (CHF) limits, while Section B outlines the regulatory thermal margin limits and fuel safety criteria that the NuScale design must meet, connecting them through the requirement that the design's protective actions (A) must satisfy the regulatory safety margins (B).

Section A cites the critical heat flux correlations report, which is based on the experimental testing described in Section B, thereby referencing the data and validation methods provided in Section B.

Section A lists a reference document on critical heat flux correlations, which provides technical background relevant to the thermal-hydraulic safety analysis described in Section B's overview of the plant's natural circulation cooling and decay heat removal systems.

Section A cites a NuScale technical report on critical heat flux correlations, which provides the technical basis for demonstrating that the NuScale design satisfies the thermal margin limits and fuel safety criteria referenced in Section B's regulatory requirements.

Section A describes the generation of new critical heat flux (CHF) data and correlations for the NuScale fuel design, while Section B lists the topical and technical reports (such as those documenting CHF testing and correlations) that are formally referenced in the NuScale FSAR, connecting the test results in A to their regulatory documentation in B.

Section A provides critical heat flux (CHF) test data and correlation development for the NuScale fuel design, which supports Section B's regulatory requirement to demonstrate that instrumentation and protective actions prevent violation of CHF limits and ensure thermal-hydraulic stability.

Section A provides critical heat flux (CHF) test data and correlation development for the NuScale fuel design, which supports Section B's regulatory requirement that reactor trip signals prevent violation of CHF limits, thereby demonstrating conformance with thermal-hydraulic stability safety margins.

Section A describes the generation and validation of CHF data and correlations for the NuFuel HTP2™ fuel design, while Section B lists the reference document where these CHF correlations are formally documented.

Section A describes specific plant features and structures, while Section B lists referenced materials and reports that provide supporting documentation for the plant's design, including those relevant to the features in Section A.

Section A lists topical reports, including those addressing quality assurance, that are referenced in the NuScale FSAR, while Section B discusses how the FSAR demonstrates conformance with DSRS 15.9.A requirements for thermal hydraulic stability, indicating that the referenced reports in A provide supporting documentation for the regulatory claims made in B.

Section A lists the topical reports and technical documents referenced in the NuScale FSAR, some of which provide the technical basis for the safety approaches described in Section B regarding thermal hydraulic stability and regulatory conformance.

Section A lists topical reports incorporated by reference in the FSAR, while Section B provides a specific reference to one of these reports (the Critical Heat Flux Correlations), connecting them through the documentation of supporting technical information.

Section A discusses the requirement to demonstrate stability-related instrumentation functionality by analysis, while Section B addresses the need for backup options if primary licensing solutions are inoperable, both focusing on ensuring thermal hydraulic stability in the NuScale design.

Section A discusses the need to demonstrate thermal hydraulic stability and prevent CHF violations, while Section B lists a reference document that provides the critical heat flux correlations used in the NuScale design, directly supporting the analysis required in Section A.

Section A discusses how the NuScale design avoids the need for exclusion zone methodology by relying on reactor trip signals to prevent CHF violations, while Section B references the technical report that provides the critical heat flux correlations used to determine those CHF limits.
